title: strychnos neglecta
intro: Strychnos neglecta is a species of plant in the family Loganiaceae, commonly found in tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called strychnine. However, the leaves and roots of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The herbal preparations of Strychnos neglecta include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to harness its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
